stem: The function h(t) = -16t2 + b estimates an object's height, in feet, above the ground t seconds after the object is dropped, where b is a constant. The function estimates that the object is 1,936 feet above the ground when it is dropped at t = 0. Approximately how many seconds after being dropped does the function estimate the object will hit the ground?
rationale: Choice D is correct. Substituting t = 0 into the function gives 1,936 = b, so h(t) = -16t2 + 1,936. The object hits the ground when h(t) = 0: -16t2 + 1,936 = 0, so t2 = 121 and t = 11 (taking the positive root). The other choices result from arithmetic errors or failing to take the square root.
